steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's goal is to contrast the two artworks. A contrast requires placing two different things side by side and highlighting how they differ. The key distinguishing fact from the notes is that Kiefer's prize-winning work is a painting and Arp's is a sculpture.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Kiefer's Midgard is a painting, while Arp's Sculpture Classique is a sculpture.: Directly contrasts the two artworks by medium using 'while.' ✓
  • Choice B — Kiefer won a Carnegie Prize gold medal in 1985 for the painting Midgard.: Focuses only on Kiefer — no contrast with Arp's artwork. ✗
  • Choice C — Carnegie Prize gold medals have been awarded to both painters and sculptors.: A general statement about the prize; does not contrast the two specific artworks. ✗
  • Choice D — Both Midgard and Sculpture Classique are Carnegie Prize gold medal-winning artworks.: Highlights similarity (both won gold medals), not contrast. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
